skill.name: backend-architect
skill.description: Guides backend architecture decisions for APIs, microservices, event-driven systems, service boundaries, resilience, observability, and rollout planning.
skill.summary: Design scalable backend services, APIs, and distributed system architecture.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported weak cryptography, Windows SAM, hidden file access, and reconnaissance patterns, but these are false positives from architecture terminology in SKILL.md. No executable code, network behavior, secret access, prompt injection attempt, or data exfiltration intent was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Terms
low_findings.description: Static detections at the listed lines are references to backend architecture concepts such as API design, documentation, distributed systems, and examples. They do not instruct the assistant to use weak cryptography or execute security-sensitive code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are Markdown descriptions and bullet points about backend architecture. No weak cryptographic algorithm is recommended or implemented.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Windows SAM Database
low_findings.description: The static hit is caused by the word SAML in an SSO integration bullet. It refers to identity federation, not the Windows SAM credential database.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 102 explicitly discusses SAML, OAuth providers, and identity federation. There is no reference to Windows credential stores or file access.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Hidden File Access
low_findings.description: The static hit is caused by the .NET framework name in a technology list. It is not a path, hidden file, or filesystem access instruction.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 187 is a framework expertise bullet for C# and .NET. The skill contains no file read, write, traversal, or hidden file access behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The static hit appears on a workflow description stating that backend services can be built on a solid data foundation. It does not request host, network, or environment enumeration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is plain architectural guidance. No command, API, or instruction performs system reconnaissance.

content.value_statement: Backend design choices can become hard to change after services launch. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code plan APIs, service boundaries, resilience, observability, and rollout strategy.
actual_capabilities: Defines backend service boundaries and responsibilities.
Plans REST, GraphQL, gRPC, WebSocket, and webhook API patterns.
Recommends synchronous and asynchronous service communication models.
Designs resilience patterns such as retries, timeouts, and circuit breakers.
Outlines observability, testing, documentation, and rollout strategies.
Compares backend frameworks and architecture trade-offs.
limitations: Does not implement full production code by itself.
Does not replace a security audit or compliance review.
Defers detailed database schema design to a database-focused skill.
Needs project context, scale targets, and constraints for strong recommendations.

use_cases.title: Plan a New Product API
use_cases.description: Define endpoints, contracts, authentication, rate limits, observability, and rollout steps before implementation begins.

use_cases.title: Split a Monolith Safely
use_cases.description: Identify service boundaries, migration phases, integration patterns, and operational risks for gradual decomposition.

use_cases.title: Design Event-Driven Workflows
use_cases.description: Map producers, consumers, topics, retries, dead letter queues, idempotency, and monitoring for asynchronous systems.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Basic API Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning for a new backend service
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a backend API for this product idea: [describe product]. Include service responsibilities, main endpoints, authentication, and key risks.

prompt_templates.title: Define Service Boundaries
prompt_templates.scenario: Team needs clear ownership before implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Given these business capabilities and constraints: [list them], propose service boundaries, data ownership, communication patterns, and trade-offs.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Resilient Microservice Architecture
prompt_templates.scenario: System must handle scale and partial failures
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a microservices architecture for [domain]. Include sync and async communication, failure handling, observability, deployment strategy, and testing.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Backend Architecture Decision
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of an existing proposal
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this backend architecture proposal: [paste proposal]. Identify boundary issues, scalability risks, operational gaps, security concerns, and better alternatives.

output_examples.input: Design an order management backend for an e-commerce platform.
output: A service map separating orders, payments, inventory, shipping, and notifications.
API contract guidance for order creation, status tracking, cancellation, and webhook events.
Resilience guidance for payment retries, idempotency keys, dead letter queues, and tracing.

output_examples.input: Plan a migration from a monolith to microservices.
output: A phased strangler migration plan with candidate boundaries and dependencies.
Risk notes for shared databases, distributed transactions, observability, and team ownership.
Rollout guidance using canary releases, backward-compatible contracts, and rollback checkpoints.
A recommendation for WebSocket or GraphQL subscription patterns with connection scaling notes.
State synchronization, event ordering, authorization, and replay considerations.
Monitoring guidance for latency, dropped connections, fan-out pressure, and error rates.

best_practices: Provide business goals, scale targets, latency needs, and consistency requirements before asking for architecture.
Ask for trade-offs when choosing between REST, GraphQL, gRPC, queues, and event streams.
Request operational details such as observability, rollout, testing, and failure handling.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for microservices before defining domain boundaries and team ownership.
Do not skip authentication, authorization, rate limiting, and input validation in API plans.
Do not treat generated architecture as final without review by engineers who know the system.

faq.question: Can this skill design REST and GraphQL APIs?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers REST, GraphQL, gRPC, WebSockets, server-sent events, and webhooks.

faq.question: Can it choose between monolith and microservices?
faq.answer: Yes. It can compare architecture options using scale, complexity, team ownership, and operational cost.

faq.question: Does it write production backend code?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on architecture, contracts, patterns, risks, and implementation guidance.

faq.question: Does it handle database schema design?
faq.answer: Only at a boundary level. Detailed schema design should use a database-focused review.

faq.question: Is this useful for existing systems?
faq.answer: Yes. It can review proposals, migration plans, service boundaries, and operational gaps.

faq.question: What context should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide domain goals, users, expected traffic, latency needs, data consistency needs, cloud constraints, and team structure.
